name: Red Fort
description: A historic fort in the city of Delhi that served as the main residence of the Mughal Emperors.
location: Netaji Subhash Marg, Chandni Chowk, New Delhi, Delhi 110006

name: India Gate
description: An arch-memorial dedicated to the soldiers of the British Indian Army who died in World War I.
location: Rajpath, India Gate, New Delhi, Delhi 110001

name: Qutub Minar
description: A minaret that forms part of the Qutb complex, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
location: Qutb Minar, Mehrauli, New Delhi, Delhi 110030

name: Lotus Temple
description: A Bahá'í House of Worship notable for its flowerlike shape.
location: Lotus Temple Rd, Bahapur, Shambhu Dayal Bagh, Kalkaji, New Delhi, Delhi 110019

name: Humayun's Tomb
description: The tomb of the Mughal Emperor Humayun, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
location: Mathura Rd, Nizamuddin East, New Delhi, Delhi 110013

name: Akshardham Temple
description: A Hindu temple and spiritual-cultural campus dedicated to devotion, learning and harmony.
location: Noida Mor, Pandav Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi 110092

name: Lodhi Garden
description: A city park containing Mohammed Shah's Tomb, Tomb of Sikandar Lodi, Shisha Ghat and Bara Gumbad.

name: Hauz Khas Village
description: A historic urban village known for its medieval monuments, trendy boutiques, art galleries and cafes.

name: Chandni Chowk
description: One of the oldest and busiest markets in Old Delhi, famous for its spices, dried fruit, silver jewelry and vivid saris.
location: Chandni Chowk, Near Red Fort, New Delhi, Delhi 110006

name: India Habitat Centre
description: A multidisciplinary habitat providing a liberal, pro-active, and dynamic environment for a variety of individuals and institutions working in diverse habitat-related areas.
